summary.intent: Implement native filesystem tools (Read, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Glob, Grep) for the moltis project to fix issue #657, which identified a model-quality regression and security gap caused by routing all file operations through the exec tool.
summary.outcome: Phase 1 MVP implemented and committed: six native Rust fs tools in crates/tools/src/fs/ with Claude-exact schemas, 751 tests passing, registered in gateway, with 5 follow-up discovered-from beads filed for deferred gaps.

repo: AgentTool trait is defined in crates/agents/src/tool_registry.rs:12 with name/description/parameters_schema/execute methods
ExecTool at crates/tools/src/exec.rs:194 (~1.7K LOC) is the canonical template for new tools — already wired to ApprovalManager, SandboxRouter, NodeExecProvider, and policy.rs
file_io.rs is an internal helper only (used by send_image/send_document), not an agent-facing tool — it has a 20MB cap for read_host_file/read_sandbox_file
Policy profiles in policy.rs:33 are name-based glob matching; the 'coding' profile only allowed ['exec', 'browser', 'memory'] — new tool names must be explicitly added
Tools are registered in crates/gateway/src/server.rs:3575 next to CalcTool
moltis_metrics pipeline already provides moltis_tool_executions_total and moltis_tool_execution_errors_total — no new custom counters needed
just lint fails on a pre-existing llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA build error unrelated to fs tools changes
Workspace uses nightly-2025-11-30 for fmt and clippy per CLAUDE.md rules

code.finding: ExecTool — canonical template for new tools, wired to ApprovalManager/SandboxRouter/policy
Tool registration point — new register_fs_tools() call slots in here next to CalcTool
New pub mod fs; added here to expose the fs tools module
format_numbered_lines must trim \r before rendering to handle CRLF files — fixed post-audit
coding profile hardcodes allowed tool names — must be updated when adding new tools or they're silently blocked

workflow: Reference repo survey pattern: claw-code was catalog-only (taxonomy/naming), openclaw best for edge-case semantics (adaptive paging, edit recovery, param compat), hermes best for agent-loop concerns (re-read tracking, path policy) but still shells out
Claude-exact parameter naming from day 1 avoids integration cost — openclaw paid real cost with CLAUDE_PARAM_GROUPS and patchToolSchemaForClaudeCompatibility after the fact
Self-audit after implementation caught 15 gaps including policy profile breakage, CRLF handling, relative path resolution, and missing contract tests
bd dolt pull failed with 'remote origin not found' at session start — beads may be out of sync with shared store
Discovered-from beads should be filed for gaps surfaced during implementation, not just planned phases
Cargo.lock must be verified in sync before commit per CLAUDE.md local-validate.sh requirement

friction: bd dolt pull failed at session start (remote 'origin' not found) — no way to fix locally, left beads potentially out of sync
Initial 'policy is free' claim was incorrect — coding profile silently blocks new tools unless names are explicitly added, caught only during post-implementation audit
just lint is broken workspace-wide due to pre-existing llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA build error, making it impossible to use the standard lint recipe
infer crate was initially added as a workspace dep for binary detection then removed after switching to a null-byte heuristic — wasted a dependency cycle
Stale task reminder notifications (bbevbh4if) appeared mid-session from a background task that had already been handled
open_items: Phase 2 (moltis-sug): FsBridge trait for sandbox routing, tar round-trip for Edit/Write in sandbox, native Read/Glob/Grep sandbox bridge
Phase 3 (moltis-8o1): adaptive paging (port openclaw formula), edit whitespace-recovery heuristic, re-read loop detection (port hermes tracker), must-read-before-write invariant
Phase 4 (moltis-6co): [tools.fs] config section with allow_paths/deny_paths/workspace_only/binary_policy/follow_symlinks, hook payload docs, hardening
Working directory for Glob/Grep defaults to process cwd — needs workspace root plumbed through builder like ExecTool (filed as discovered-from bead)
Relative path handling in Read/Write/Edit resolves against server cwd — should error loudly on relative paths (filed as discovered-from bead)
No total output size cap on Read — could blow payload limits (filed as discovered-from bead)
No contract test for register_fs_tools through ToolRegistry pipeline (filed as discovered-from bead)
No fs-tools Cargo feature flag for opt-out in crates/cli/Cargo.toml as promised in phase 1 bead (filed as discovered-from bead)
Session-key/_workspace_root not plumbed through fs tools yet — needed for phase 3 read tracker, will require retrofit of six tools (filed as discovered-from bead)
UTF-8 BOM stripping not implemented — BOM renders as \u{feff} on line 1
Read follows symlinks silently — behavior is intentional (matches Claude Code) but undocumented
